infobox.value: (**BITS Pilani**) is a private [deemed university](/wiki/Deemed_university "Deemed university") in [Pilani](/wiki/Pilani "Pilani"), [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"), India.[[17]](#cite_note-17) It focuses primarily on higher education and research in engineering and sciences.[[18]](#cite_note-18) BITS Pilani was one of the first six institutes in India to be declared [Institutions of Eminence](/wiki/Institute_of_Eminence "Institute of Eminence").[[19]](#cite_note-BITS_Pilani,_IIT_Delhi_and_IIT_Bombay_among_the_six_'Institutes_of_Eminence'-19) According to 2012 data, BITS Pilani has an acceptance rate (on-campus) of 1.47%, making it one of the most exclusive [technical universities](/wiki/Institute_of_technology "Institute of technology") in the world.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

The **Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani** (**BITS Pilani**) is a private [deemed university](/wiki/Deemed_university "Deemed university") in [Pilani](/wiki/Pilani "Pilani"), [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"), India.[[17]](#cite_note-17) It focuses primarily on higher education and research in engineering and sciences.[[18]](#cite_note-18) BITS Pilani was one of the first six institutes in India to be declared [Institutions of Eminence](/wiki/Institute_of_Eminence "Institute of Eminence").[[19]](#cite_note-BITS_Pilani,_IIT_Delhi_and_IIT_Bombay_among_the_six_'Institutes_of_Eminence'-19) According to 2012 data, BITS Pilani has an acceptance rate (on-campus) of 1.47%, making it one of the most exclusive [technical universities](/wiki/Institute_of_technology "Institute of technology") in the world.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

The institute was established in its present form in 1964.[[22]](#cite_note-22) During this period, the institute's transformation from a regional engineering college to a national university was backed by [G.D. Birla](/wiki/G.D._Birla "G.D. Birla"). The university has expanded its campuses from Pilani to [Dubai](/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology_and_Science,_Pilani_%E2%80%93_Dubai_Campus "Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Dubai Campus"), [Goa](/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology_and_Science,_Pilani_%E2%80%93_Goa_Campus "Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Goa Campus"), [Hyderabad](/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology_and_Science,_Pilani_%E2%80%93_Hyderabad_Campus "Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Hyderabad Campus") and [Mumbai](/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology_and_Science,_Pilani_%E2%80%93_Mumbai_Campus "Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Mumbai Campus").[[23]](#cite_note-23) After expansion to a campus in Dubai, it has become the first international deemed university, spearheading research in science and engineering with four established campuses and fifteen academic departments. Backed by the [Aditya Birla Group](/wiki/Aditya_Birla_Group "Aditya Birla Group"), the institute secures extramural research funds from industries and various government agencies.[[24]](#cite_note-auto-24)[[25]](#cite_note-auto1-25)

Admissions to on-campus programs are solely merit-based and assessed by the entrance examinations conducted by BITS.[[26]](#cite_note-26) It is one of the few institutions in India that do not have any [reservation](/wiki/Reservation_in_India "Reservation in India") policies in their admission criteria.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Inception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Inception")]

[![Large buildings surrounding green quadrangle](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/BITS-Pilani_campus_aerial_view.jpg/250px-BITS-Pilani_campus_aerial_view.jpg)](/wiki/File:BITS-Pilani_campus_aerial_view.jpg)

Aerial view, BITS Pilani (1978)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Aerial_View_BITS_Pilani%2C_2014.png/250px-Aerial_View_BITS_Pilani%2C_2014.png)](/wiki/File:Aerial_View_BITS_Pilani,_2014.png)

Aerial image of the Pilani campus with the newly inaugurated Rotunda (2014)

Founded in 1929, the Birla Education Trust established an intermediate college,[[27]](#cite_note-27) followed by the addition of Science and Pharmacy colleges in 1943 and 1950 respectively.[[28]](#cite_note-28) The Birla College of Engineering, offering degree programs in electrical and mechanical engineering was started in 1946.[[29]](#cite_note-29) The master's program in electronics was introduced in 1955.[[30]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-30) In 1964, the Birla Colleges of Science, Engineering and Pharmacy were incorporated to form the Birla Institute of Technology and Science (BITS).

Reacting to early criticism about the project, contemporary advisor Thomas Drew said:

> In my judgment to attempt to develop an American institution in India would be like trying to graft apples on a pine tree. We have not been asked to make such an attempt. We were asked to help devise in India an Indian technological school to produce graduates with the know-how to produce knowledge pertinent for India…. In many respects, they consider us immature, rude, hypocritical barbarians who in certain respects happened to hit it lucky. To be viable in India an institution must be framed with Indian values in mind.[[31]](#cite_note-HSBITS-31)

BITS Pilani became a [deemed university](/wiki/Deemed_university "Deemed university") established under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956 by notification No. F.12-23/63.U-2 of 18 June 1964.[[32]](#cite_note-HP-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) In its formative years, the Institute tied up with the [Massachusetts Institute of Technology](/wiki/Massachusetts_Institute_of_Technology "Massachusetts Institute of Technology") (MIT), Boston, USA.[[32]](#cite_note-HP-32)[[34]](#cite_note-34) It adopted a semester system, modular structure of courses, continuous and internal evaluation, letter grading and the likes. It also created linkages with the industries which yielded structured “Practice Schools” as an integral component of education.[[32]](#cite_note-HP-32)

The Institute board provided direction in developing a curriculum, selecting equipment, upgrading the library and recruiting (and training) an Indian faculty. To quicken the pace of reform he convinced [C. R. Mitra](/wiki/C._R._Mitra "C. R. Mitra") to be the new director of the institute. Mitra advocated a "practice school" internship program as a requirement for faculty and students.[[35]](#cite_note-35) The Practice School Program is still a requirement for students in BITS.

[![Low, gold-colored building seen from green space](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/Bits_Clock_tower.jpg/250px-Bits_Clock_tower.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bits_Clock_tower.jpg)

Clock tower, BITS Pilani

According to American historians Robert Kargon and Stuart Leslie:

> BITS offered an opportunity to build a leading technological university in India responsive to India's goals, to produce practising engineers who will be in a position to graduate and to build industries in India, under Indian conditions. With its emphasis on the Practice School and ties to Indian industry, it helped educate Indian industrialists along with Indian engineers who would remain in India, in contrast to many other engineering colleges in India, most of whose graduates would leave the country after obtaining their basic engineering education. The Ford Foundation Evaluators...proudly noted that the Indian government, despite having given no direct financial support, was looking to BITS to provide a model for future development in education in engineering and science in India.[[31]](#cite_note-HSBITS-31)

Like [Berkeley](/wiki/1960s_Berkeley_protests "1960s Berkeley protests"), BITS Pilani has also experienced student activism. BITS Pilani had to be shut down multiple times because of student strikes in 1973, 1980, 1985 and 2017.[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37)

In 1999, enrollment expanded from 2,500 to 4,000[[38]](#cite_note-KKB-38) and campuses were founded in [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai") (2000) and [Goa](/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology_and_Science,_Pilani_%E2%80%93_Goa_Campus "Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Goa Campus") (2004). Upon invitation by the [Government of Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Government_of_Andhra_Pradesh "Government of Andhra Pradesh"), BITS Pilani acquired 200 acres (81 ha) of land from the [Andhra Pradesh](/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") government through the [Hyderabad Urban Development Authority](/wiki/Hyderabad_Urban_Development_Authority "Hyderabad Urban Development Authority") for a new constituent campus in 2006. The land is located in Jawaharnagar, [Shamirpet](/wiki/Shamirpet "Shamirpet") Mandal in the [Hyderabad Metropolitan Region](/wiki/Hyderabad_Metropolitan_Region "Hyderabad Metropolitan Region").[[39]](#cite_note-39) The BITS Pilani Hyderabad campus was opened in 2008.

BITS School of Management (BITSoM) was established in January 2021 and offers a two-year full-time residential MBA program.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Alongside BITS Law School (BITSLAW), launched in March 2023, and BITS Design School (BITSDES), launched in February 2024, all three schools initially operated from interim campuses in Mumbai.[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42) In 2024, they moved to a newly inaugurated 63-acre permanent campus in [Kalyan](/wiki/Kalyan "Kalyan"), within the [Mumbai Metropolitan Region](/wiki/Mumbai_Metropolitan_Region "Mumbai Metropolitan Region").[[43]](#cite_note-43)

### Chancellor and academic head

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Chancellor and academic head")]

BITS Pilani has a tradition of long-serving chancellors. Its founder, G.D. Birla, was chancellor from the college's inception until his death in 1983.[[38]](#cite_note-KKB-38) He was followed by his son, [Krishna Kumar Birla](/wiki/Krishna_Kumar_Birla "Krishna Kumar Birla"), who was chancellor until his death in 2008.[[44]](#cite_note-44) Currently, [Kumar Mangalam Birla](/wiki/Kumar_Mangalam_Birla "Kumar Mangalam Birla") is chancellor and Shobhana Bhartia is pro-chancellor.[[45]](#cite_note-KMB-45)

The first academic head of the institution was J. C. Stracliff (Principal) for a period of 3 years during 1946–1949.[[46]](#cite_note-46) V. Lakshminarayan became Principal of Birla Engineering College in 1949 and Director of BITS Pilani in 1964 when it was established (serving until 1969).[[47]](#cite_note-47) He was succeeded by the third and fourth academic heads (BITS Pilani Directors) C.R. Mitra (1969–1989)[[48]](#cite_note-48) and S. Venkateswaran (1989–2006). L.K. Maheshwari was the fifth academic head, serving as Director as well as Vice Chancellor, the latter terminology denoting the overall head of multiple campuses (each of which now has a Director). The current directors of the campuses are Sudhirkumar Barai, Suman Kundu, Soumyo Mukherji, and Souri Banerjee for Pilani, Goa, Hyderabad, and Dubai campuses, respectively.[[49]](#cite_note-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51)[[52]](#cite_note-52)

In April 2016, Chancellor KM Birla announced that Souvik Bhattacharya of [IIT Kharagpur](/wiki/IIT_Kharagpur "IIT Kharagpur") was selected to take over as the new Vice chancellor of BITS Pilani. He took over in June 2016 to become the 6th academic head of the institute.[[53]](#cite_note-BNJ-1-53) Ranendra N. Saha served as the Acting Vice Chancellor from 13 June 2021 to 30 September 2021 after the expiration of Bhattacharya's term. The latter was re-appointed as the Vice Chancellor from 1 October 2021 for a period of five years.[[54]](#cite_note-54) On 10 March 2023, [V. Ramgopal Rao](/wiki/V._Ramgopal_Rao "V. Ramgopal Rao") took over the Vice Chancellor's office from the outgoing Bhattacharya, becoming the seventh academic head of the institute.[[55]](#cite_note-55)[[56]](#cite_note-56)

## Campuses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Campuses")]

### Pilani Campus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Pilani Campus")]

Spread across 328 acres (1,330,000 m2), Pilani campus is positioned 200 kilometres (120 mi) west of Delhi and 220 kilometres (140 mi) north of Jaipur.[[57]](#cite_note-Visit_BITS-57) The campus has 11,245 square metres (121,040 sq ft) of classrooms and 7,069 square metres (76,090 sq ft) of laboratories.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/06/GD_Birla_Memorial.jpg/250px-GD_Birla_Memorial.jpg)](/wiki/File:GD_Birla_Memorial.jpg)

G.D. Birla Memorial

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d3/Birla_Science_Museum%2C_BITS_Pilani.jpg/250px-Birla_Science_Museum%2C_BITS_Pilani.jpg)](/wiki/File:Birla_Science_Museum,_BITS_Pilani.jpg)

Birla Science Centre, BITS Pilani

The Pilani campus has India's first technological museum, the Birla Museum. Built in 1954, it showcases technological achievements.[[58]](#cite_note-58)[[59]](#cite_note-59) BITS has a 2,535-square-metre (27,290 sq ft) auditorium decorated with paintings by students from the department of art and decoration.[[60]](#cite_note-BITS_Pilani_Statistics-60)

[![Temple with four rounded step levels](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b6/Saraswati_temple%2C_BITS_Pilani.jpg/250px-Saraswati_temple%2C_BITS_Pilani.jpg)](/wiki/File:Saraswati_temple,_BITS_Pilani.jpg)

[Saraswati](/wiki/Saraswati "Saraswati") temple, BITS Pilani

BITS has a [Sharda Peeth](https://bet.org.in/instutions/sharda-peeth-pilani/) dedicated to the goddess [Saraswati](/wiki/Saraswati "Saraswati"), Sharda Peeth, built by G. D. Birla. The white marble temple is built on a 7-foot (2.1 m)-high foundation, with 70 pillars for support. It covers an area of 25,000 square feet (2,300 m2).[[61]](#cite_note-61)

#### Residential and dining facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Residential and dining facilities")]

The institute has fourteen hostels in total. Each hostel is referred as a "Bhawan", the [Sanskrit](/wiki/Sanskrit "Sanskrit") word for hostel. Thirteen of them – [Krishna](/wiki/Krishna "Krishna") Bhawan, [Vishwakarma](/wiki/Vishvakarma "Vishvakarma") Bhawan, [Rana Pratap](/wiki/Maharana_Pratap "Maharana Pratap") Bhawan, [Bhagirath](/wiki/Bhagiratha "Bhagiratha") Bhawan, [Ashok](/wiki/Ashoka "Ashoka") Bhawan, [Gandhi](/wiki/Mahatma_Gandhi "Mahatma Gandhi") Bhawan, [Shankar](/wiki/Shiva "Shiva") Bhawan, [Vyas](/wiki/Vyasa "Vyasa") Bhawan, [Ram](/wiki/Rama "Rama") Bhawan, [Budh](/wiki/The_Buddha "The Buddha") Bhawan, [Malviya](/wiki/Madan_Mohan_Malaviya "Madan Mohan Malaviya") Bhawan, [Sir CV Raman](/wiki/C._V._Raman "C. V. Raman") Bhavan and [Srinivasa Ramanujan](/wiki/Srinivasa_Ramanujan "Srinivasa Ramanujan") Bhawan, are for male students. [Meera](/wiki/Mirabai "Mirabai") Bhawan is the only hostel complex for female students. The hostels are named after saints, scientists, and historical and religious figures. The institute also has a hostel for parents and guests.[[62]](#cite_note-Facilities-62)

Every two hostels share a mess hall except Meera Bhawan, Srinivasa Ramanujan Bhawan and Sir CV Raman Bhawan, which have their own mess halls. All dining areas are student-managed.[[63]](#cite_note-63) Students may also eat at a "Redi" (a small kiosk near every hostel), Institute Canteen (IC), the All-Night Canteen (ANC), Food Ministry (FM), and the Student Activity Centre (SAC) cafeteria (301 °F, Looters, and [Domino's](/wiki/Domino%27s "Domino's")). The ANC is also student-managed.[[64]](#cite_note-64) Moreover, there is a small area inside campus called "Connaught Place" which has many restaurants, cafes,[[65]](#cite_note-65) and shops for general daily needs as well.

#### Vision 2020, Mission 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Vision 2020, Mission 2012")]

In 2010 the institute launched a renewal project, "Vision 2020, Mission 2012,"[[66]](#cite_note-66) to identify and implement measures establishing BITS Pilani as one of India's top three research-led universities by 2015 and among the leading 25 technical universities in Asia by 2020. As a part of this initiative, [Kumar Mangalam Birla](/wiki/Kumar_Mangalam_Birla "Kumar Mangalam Birla") visited the Pilani campus on 13 November 2011 with pro-chancellor Shobhana Bhartia and other members of the board of governors. At this meeting, Birla announced a ₹600 crore fund for renovation and construction of new academic buildings and student hostels on the Pilani campus. The project was planned for completion in 2014.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

#### Project Vistaar

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Project Vistaar")]

In July 2025, BITS Pilani announced *Project Vistaar*, a ₹2,200 crore expansion—the largest investment in its history.[[68]](#cite_note-68) The initiative includes a new AI+ campus in [Amaravati](/wiki/Amaravati "Amaravati") and a ₹1,219 crore upgrade of existing campuses in Pilani, Goa, and Hyderabad. The announcement was made by Chancellor Kumar Mangalam Birla during the institute’s convocation ceremony.[[69]](#cite_note-69)

The Amaravati campus will serve as a hub for advanced programs in AI, machine learning, innovation, and strategy.[[70]](#cite_note-70) Developed in two phases, it will accommodate over 7,000 students and offer global collaborations, doctoral programs, and sustainable infrastructure.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

## Academics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Academics")]

### Academic programmes (On-campus)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Academic programmes (On-campus)")]

#### Integrated first degrees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Integrated first degrees")]

BITS Pilani offers four-year integrated [first-degree](/wiki/Undergraduate_degree "Undergraduate degree") programs in engineering and pharmacy, and integrated Master of Science programs in science and technology.[[72]](#cite_note-72) The Dual-Degree program allows students to pursue two degrees, namely a Master's in Science (M.Sc.) and a Bachelor's in Engineering (B.E.) simultaneously for a time duration of five-years. The dual-degree program is part of the first-degree programs and is offered to students on the basis of BITSAT scores.[[73]](#cite_note-73)

#### Higher degrees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Higher degrees")]

BITS Pilani offers two-year [higher-degree](/wiki/Postgraduate_education "Postgraduate education") programs in engineering, pharmacy and business administration.[[74]](#cite_note-BITS_Pilani-74) The Master of Engineering (M.E.) program is offered to students on the basis of [GATE](/wiki/Graduate_Aptitude_Test_in_Engineering "Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering") scores and BITS HD exam scores.[[75]](#cite_note-:0-75)

**Doctoral degrees**

BITS Pilani offers Ph.D. programs in Engineering, Sciences, Pharmacy, Social Sciences, and Management across its campuses. Admission is based on a written test and interview, with exemptions for candidates holding higher degrees (M.Phil./M.E.) or national-level fellowships like CSIR NET-JRF, DBT-JRF, or UGC NET-JRF.[[76]](#cite_note-76)[[77]](#cite_note-77)

### Admission

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Admission")]

#### Pilani, Goa and Hyderabad campuses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Pilani, Goa and Hyderabad campuses")]

Before 2005, admissions were based on the candidates' score in the 12th board examination. BITS had been moderating marks from various school boards since 1982.

Since 2005, admission to first-degree in BITS has been offered on the basis of the student's performance in the all-India entrance examination, the Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test (BITSAT).[[78]](#cite_note-toi2012-78) BITSAT, for which applications are submitted in December, is conducted online in May and June in cities all over India. The exam tests the candidate's knowledge, reasoning and analytical abilities in [physics](/wiki/Physics "Physics"), [chemistry](/wiki/Chemistry "Chemistry"), mathematics, [English](/wiki/English_studies "English studies") and [logical reasoning](/wiki/Logical_reasoning "Logical reasoning"), and is based on higher secondary curricula in India and abroad. A 2012 news report showed that [BITSAT](https://www.collegecourses.in/bitsat/) had become more competitive than the [IIT-JEE](/wiki/IIT-JEE "IIT-JEE"), in terms of the ratio of the number of aspirants to the number of seats available.[[78]](#cite_note-toi2012-78)

Admission to on-campus higher degrees such as Master's in Engineering (M.E.) is done on the basis of [GATE](/wiki/Graduate_Aptitude_Test_in_Engineering "Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering") scores and BITS HD (Higher Degree) examination scores.[[75]](#cite_note-:0-75)

For foreign students, BITS has a separate admission scheme called International Student Admissions (ISA) category. To gauge the merit of these ISA students, BITS accepts the [SAT](/wiki/SAT "SAT") and [SAT Subject Tests](/wiki/SAT_Subject_Tests "SAT Subject Tests") in chemistry, physics, and Math 2C.[[79]](#cite_note-BITS_website-79)

#### Dubai campus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Dubai campus")]

Admission to [Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Dubai Campus](/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology_and_Science,_Pilani_%E2%80%93_Dubai_Campus "Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani – Dubai Campus") is based on scores in the 12th standard qualifying exam along with BITSAT. Although the BITS Pilani-Dubai campus was established for the educational requirements of the [Gulf Cooperation Council](/wiki/Gulf_Cooperation_Council "Gulf Cooperation Council") countries, admission is open to students of all nationalities.[[80]](#cite_note-80)

### Academic and industrial collaborations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Academic and industrial collaborations")]

BITS Pilani collaborates extensively with industries and government organizations, including the [DST](/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)"), [ICMR](/wiki/ICMR "ICMR"), [DBT](/wiki/Department_of_Biotechnology "Department of Biotechnology"), [ISRO](/wiki/ISRO "ISRO"), [DRDO](/wiki/Defence_Research_and_Development_Organisation "Defence Research and Development Organisation") and [DAE](/wiki/Department_of_Atomic_Energy "Department of Atomic Energy").[[24]](#cite_note-auto-24)[[81]](#cite_note-81) The institute actively promotes innovation and supports ventures by faculty, students, and alumni through its incubation programs.[[25]](#cite_note-auto1-25)[[82]](#cite_note-82) BITS is a partner in developing the JournalServer[[83]](#cite_note-BITS_JS3-83) open-access digital library, Project [IPV6](/wiki/IPV6 "IPV6")[[84]](#cite_note-BITS_IPV63-84) and the MIT iCampus[[85]](#cite_note-BITS_iCampus3-85) initiative. BITS Pilani has signed key MoUs with institutions such as the [Indian Institute of Science](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science "Indian Institute of Science") (IISc), [Tata Institute of Fundamental Research](/wiki/Tata_Institute_of_Fundamental_Research "Tata Institute of Fundamental Research") (TIFR) and [IIT Bombay](/wiki/IIT_Bombay "IIT Bombay") for joint research and specialized courses.[[86]](#cite_note-86)[[87]](#cite_note-87)[[88]](#cite_note-88) The [One Health Trust](/wiki/One_Health_Trust "One Health Trust"), in collaboration with BITS, has a joint research program in Data Sciences for Global Health, focusing on research training, fieldwork, and data analysis.[[89]](#cite_note-89)[[90]](#cite_note-90) BITS Pilani has signed MoUs with the [University of Southern California](/wiki/University_of_Southern_California "University of Southern California"), [University at Buffalo](/wiki/University_at_Buffalo "University at Buffalo"), [Iowa State University](/wiki/Iowa_State_University "Iowa State University"), [Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute](/wiki/Rensselaer_Polytechnic_Institute "Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute") in USA, [CentraleSupélec](/wiki/CentraleSup%C3%A9lec "CentraleSupélec") in France and [Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology](/wiki/Royal_Melbourne_Institute_of_Technology "Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology") (RMIT) in Australia for dual degree programmes and research initiatives.[[91]](#cite_note-91)[[92]](#cite_note-92)[[93]](#cite_note-93)[[94]](#cite_note-94)

### Research facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Research facilities")]

BITS Pilani hosts a range of advanced research facilities across its campuses, supporting interdisciplinary research and innovation in science and engineering.[[95]](#cite_note-95) The Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) at each campus provides centralized access to high-end scientific equipment.[[96]](#cite_note-96)[[97]](#cite_note-97) Other specialized facilities includes a state-of-the-art cleanroom for micro and nano-fabrication, the *Nalini Kurra Wind Tunnel Facility* for advanced research in aerodynamic and renewable energy,[[98]](#cite_note-98) the MEMS, Microfluidics & Nanoelectronics Lab (MMNE Lab) and Intracellular Parasite Education and Research Labs (iPEARL), a [BSL-2+](/wiki/Biosafety_level "Biosafety level") facility for infectious disease research.

BITS Pilani also hosts dedicated *Centres of Excellence*, including the Centre for Artificial Intelligence Research (APPCAIR), Centre of Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST), Centre of Research Excellence in National Security (CRENS), and the Advanced Research Centre for Sustainable Energy Technologies (ARCSET), which focus on key national and global research priorities.[[99]](#cite_note-99) The institute was awarded the Sophisticated Analytical and Technical Help Institute (SATHI) project by the [Department of Science and Technology](/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)") (DST), a grant of ₹60 crore to set up shared research infrastructure in areas such as material sciences, device manufacturing and biopharmaceuticals.[[100]](#cite_note-100) BITS Pilani is the only self-financed institute selected to host a Technology Innovation Hub under the Bio–Cyber Physical Systems (Bio‑CPS) vertical, through the BITS BioCyTiH Foundation, with a grant of ₹125 crore.[[101]](#cite_note-101) According to the [Nature Index](/wiki/Nature_Index "Nature Index"), BITS Pilani is currently ranked 16th among academic institutions in India in terms of research output.[[102]](#cite_note-102)

### Distance learning programmes (WILP)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Distance learning programmes (WILP)")]

BITS Pilani also offers online [distance learning](/wiki/Distance_education "Distance education") programmes such as *Work Integrated Learning Programmes (WILP)* and in which students can get a B.Tech., M.Tech., Diploma, as well as Certificate programs while working in the industry.[[103]](#cite_note-103)

### Digital education programmes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Digital education programmes")]

BITS Pilani launched its first online undergraduate degree, the B.Sc. in Computer Science, in September 2022 through a partnership with [Coursera](/wiki/Coursera "Coursera").[[104]](#cite_note-104) The programme offeris flexible pacing and multiple exit options. In 2025, BITS Pilani established *BITS Pilani Digital* as a dedicated platform to expand its online degree offerings. The initial offerings include Bachelors and Masters in Data Science & AI.[[105]](#cite_note-105)

## Student activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Student activities")]

### Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Events")]

#### Oasis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Oasis")]

Main article: [Oasis (festival)](/wiki/Oasis_(festival) "Oasis (festival)")

[Oasis](/wiki/Oasis_(festival) "Oasis (festival)") is the annual [cultural festival](/wiki/Cultural_festival "Cultural festival") of the BITS, Pilani. It hosts a variety of events in various categories like dance, drama, literature, comedy, fashion and music (such as [Rocktaves](/wiki/Rocktaves "Rocktaves")).[[123]](#cite_note-123)

#### BOSM

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: BOSM")]

Main article: [BOSM (festival)](/wiki/BOSM_(festival) "BOSM (festival)")

[BOSM](/wiki/BOSM_(festival) "BOSM (festival)") (BITS Open Sports Meet) is the annual sports competition at the Pilani campus. BITS Pilani invites colleges throughout India to participate in events including carrom board, hockey, [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket"), basketball, football, volleyball, [track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field"), badminton, tennis, table tennis, squash and weightlifting. Since its 2010 silver jubilee (25th) anniversary, BOSM has invited a team from Moratuwa University in Sri Lanka.[[124]](#cite_note-124)

#### APOGEE

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: APOGEE")]

APOGEE (A Professions Oriented Gathering over Educational Experiences) is an international annual technical festival at the Pilani campus.[[125]](#cite_note-125) Since its inception in 1983, APOGEE has hosted students and researchers from across the country. The guest lecture series of the fest, Think Again Conclave has received eminent speakers in the past which include [A.P.J. Abdul Kalam](/wiki/A._P._J._Abdul_Kalam "A. P. J. Abdul Kalam"), [Richard Stallman](/wiki/Richard_Stallman "Richard Stallman"), [Jimmy Wales](/wiki/Jimmy_Wales "Jimmy Wales"), [Kailash Satyarthi](/wiki/Kailash_Satyarthi "Kailash Satyarthi"), [Walter Lewin](/wiki/Walter_Lewin "Walter Lewin") and [A. S. Kiran Kumar](/wiki/A._S._Kiran_Kumar "A. S. Kiran Kumar").[[126]](#cite_note-126) Other events include the [Paper Presentation](/wiki/Academic_Paper "Academic Paper") Event, which is one of the oldest research paper presentation platforms in the country, and Project Presentation, which exhibits over 550 projects in various fields of [science](/wiki/Science "Science") and [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering").

### Spark

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BITS_Pilani&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Spark")]

The BITS Spark programme was launched in 2012. The programme promotes entrepreneurship courses and workshops, offers mentorship, and provides angel funds.[[127]](#cite_note-127)
